patterns.applicable_when: writing or reviewing shell scripts that use grep with alternation (|) or quantifiers (+)
patterns.context: macOS ships BSD grep which does not support GNU BRE extensions \| and \+. Scripts using these silently match nothing — gate validators appeared to work but were non-functional.
patterns.pattern: Always use grep -E (ERE) for alternation and quantifiers. Never use BRE \| or \+ in portable shell scripts.

patterns.applicable_when: shell scripts pass variables to inline Python via heredoc or -c argument
patterns.context: Shell variables interpolated into Python double-quoted heredocs allow code injection via quote boundaries. Found in 3 scripts (run-canary.sh, compare-runs.sh, and potentially hooks).
patterns.pattern: Pass dynamic values via environment variables and use single-quoted heredocs. Or use python3 -c with os.environ reads. Never interpolate shell vars into Python strings.

patterns.applicable_when: Node.js scripts use execSync with user-controlled input
patterns.context: fetch-chub.js passed user queries directly into execSync double-quoted strings. Single-quote escaping (shellEscapeSingleQuote) or execFileSync with argv arrays prevents injection.
patterns.pattern: Use shellEscapeSingleQuote for all user input in execSync, or prefer execFileSync with argv array to eliminate shell entirely.

patterns.applicable_when: scripts in .claude/skills/ need to find the project root directory
patterns.context: Skills have nested .claude/ dirs. Walking up and stopping at first .claude/ finds skill subdir, not project root. Layer 8 post-write audit was silently disabled.
patterns.pattern: Use CLAUDE.md or .claude/settings.json as sentinel for project root detection. These exist only at project root, never in skill subdirs.
